articles.title: Is the ‘Pran’ System in Venezuela’s Prisons Finished?
articles.description:
The system that delegated power in Venezuela’s prisons to pranes, or criminal leaders, seems to be ending after a series of military takeovers of jails by President Nicolás Maduro, as he eyes re-election in 2024. 
 On the morning of November 10, security forces stormed  the San Felipe Judicial Priso

articles.title: Colombian Armed Groups Continue Recruiting Children Amid Peace Talks
articles.description: On the morning of September 6, school was canceled at the José Maria Obando high school in Corinto, a small town in Cauca, Colombia. Instead of the typical bustling of 400 students chatting and filing into classrooms, there was only unsettling silence. When school employees arrived that morning, the

articles.title: Venezuelan Extortion Gangs Spark Violent Backlash in Peru
articles.description: Residents in Lima, Peru violently protested against Venezuelan extortion gangs in the city such as the Tren de Aragua cell the Gallegos. The protests suggest frustration at increasing extortion is mounting and show how Venezuelan groups have affected Lima’s criminal status quo.

articles.title: Suriname #NarcoFiles Sparks VP Response
articles.description: InSight Crime’s analysis of the links between the Surinamese government and transnational drug trafficking was picked up by the country’s main media outlets such as Suriname Herald, GFC Nieuws, and […]

articles.title: Jimmy Chérizier, alias ‘Barbecue’
articles.description: Former police officer Jimmy Chérizier, alias “Barbecue,” is one of Haiti’s most prolific gang leader and head of the G9 and family gang.

articles.title: Dozens of DEA Agents Exposed in Colombian Prosecutor’s Office Leak
articles.description: A cyber breach at Colombia’s attorney general’s office has exposed dozens of anti-drug agents in the United States, Colombia, and beyond.

articles.title: Local Police Could Disrupt Attempts to Remove Militias From Rio, Brazil
articles.description: Brazil’s government launched a crackdown on militias, and in Rio de Janeiro, the state police are needed if a real difference is to be made. The problem is that these same police are often entangled with the militias and may, in some cases, be working with the criminal groups.

articles.title: The Networks of Uruguay’s Most-Wanted Drug Trafficker
articles.description: Uruguay’s most prolific drug trafficker, Sebastian Marset, has strung together an intercontinental network of associates implicated in cocaine trafficking, money laundering, and high-profile assassinations, all while escaping the clutches of authorities in Dubai, Paraguay, and Bolivia.

articles.title: Colombia’s Total Peace May Be Unraveling
articles.description: The kidnapping of a football star’s father and other ceasefire violations have put Colombia’s Total Peace process in dire straits. Negotiations with the ELN, the EMC, and other criminal actors in Colombia are in jeopardy.

articles.title: Israeli Gangs’ Colombia Operations Detailed in Hacked AG Files

articles.title: Suriname VP, Ex-President Linked to Drug Trafficking in Colombia AG Emails
articles.description: US and Colombian investigators connected Suriname’s Vice President Ronnie Brunswijk and ex-President Desi Bouterse to recent involvement in drug trafficking, according to hacked emails that suggest ongoing high-level political links to organized crime in South America’s smallest country.

articles.title: Caribbean Profile
articles.description:
The Caribbean’s geographic location and countless islands make it a huge transshipment route for drugs heading to the United States, a dynamic that has fostered high rates of violence and gang-related crime.

articles.title: 3 Reasons Why Security Has Deteriorated in Northern Chile
articles.description: A new study has shed light on the security crisis unfolding in northern Chile, a border region pivotal to transnational crime but where state authorities maintain only a minor presence.

articles.title: Security Challenges Facing the New Mayor of Bogotá, Colombia

articles.title: Q&A: How Sand Trafficking in Brazil Became a Highly Lucrative Crime
articles.description: Brazilian authorities have launched a series of operations targeting illegal sand extraction as part of a renewed commitment to fighting environmental crimes. But much more may be required to tackle what is now a multi-billion dollar criminal enterprise.

articles.title: Colombian Mayor Explains How Gaitanistas Took Over Key Drug Trade Town
articles.description: Yefer Gamboa is the mayor of Nuquí, a city on Colombia’s Pacific coast whose beaches and tropical rainforest attract thousands of tourists each year. articles.title: The Many Violences Afflicting Haitians
articles.description: On July 7, 2021, a team of mercenaries invaded the home of Haiti’s President Jovenel Moïse and shot him dead. The country was already unstable following a series of natural disasters combined with years of political, economic, and criminal turmoil. But the assassination tipped it over the edge into

articles.title: Portrait of a ‘Terrorist’ in El Salvador
articles.description: The story of Dalila Johana Flores Flores is one of thousands of arbitrary detentions that have taken place in El Salvador. articles.title: Argentina’s Presidential Candidates Promise Hardline Responses to Rising Insecurity
articles.description: Argentines will vote for their next president on October 22. Despite coming from distinct ideological camps, the leading candidates all have staunch, tough-on-crime stances.

articles.title: Venezuela’s Most Wanted List Notable for Its Absences

articles.description: Latin American countries have raised alarms about increases in medical fentanyl seizures in recent months. While there is some cause for concern, medical fentanyl is significantly different to the illicit fentanyl that has caused a health crisis in the United States and Mexico.

articles.title: Paraguay’s Tacumbú Prison Takeover Marks Rotela Clan Resurgence
articles.description:
By taking control of Paraguay’s largest prison, the Rotela Clan flexed its muscles.

articles.title: In Strategic Colombian Port, Gangs Await Total Peace

Buenaventura is Colombia’s main port on the Pacific seaboard and one of the principal departure points for drug shipments. Long fought over by criminal groups, the city is now the scene of peace talks with the Colombian government.

articles.title: In Cauca, Colombian Rebels Make War While Talking Peace
articles.description:
Peace talks in Cauca hang by a thread after the killing of a Colombian soldier by the ex-FARC mafia, underlining the threat that groups in this strategically important drug trafficking department hold for the country’s Total Peace aims.

articles.title: Jailhouse Murders of Colombians Harden Political Rhetoric in Ecuador
articles.description:
The murder of seven Colombians suspected of involvement in the high-profile assassination of an Ecuadorian presidential candidate has seen security rhetoric drift to hard-line policies ahead of the October 15 election. 
 Six inmates held for the murder of presidential candidate Fernando Villavicenci

articles.title: Social Leaders in Cauca, Colombia Face Down Threats From Crime Groups
articles.description:
The number of leaders murdered in Cauca, Colombia, shows that their work is an obstacle for armed groups seeking to consolidate in one of the most strategic territories for organized crime in the country.

articles.title: New Money, Old Strategies in Brazil’s Anti-Crime Plan
articles.description:
Brazil has announced a new national security program to counter organized crime amid a spike in violence. But the nearly one billion reais plan rehashes many previously ineffective strategies.

articles.title: Chile Ups Efforts Against ‘Narco’ Mausoleums and Funerals
articles.description: The Chilean government has opened up a new front in the fight against drug trafficking, incorporating social and cultural approaches to the country’s insecurity crisis by targeting “narco” mausoleums and funerals.

articles.description: In the five years since Tren de Aragua first appeared in Colombia, it has built a far-reaching regional network, establishing a permanent presence in Colombia, Peru, and Chile, with further reports of their presence in Ecuador, Brazil, and Bolivia.

articles.description: Homicides are higher than ever in Costa Rica as authorities struggle to find the coordination required to tackle issue. Drug trafficking and organized crime are behind the climbing murder rate, yet efforts to tackle these issues have so far been ineffective.

articles.description: Copycat gangs are increasingly posing as Venezuela’s Tren de Aragua to extort victims in Peru and Chile.

articles.description: Videos and pamphlets allegedly published by the Gaitanistas in Bogotá have made Venezuela’s Tren de Aragua a target.

articles.description: Reports of an assassination plot against Guatemala’s president-elect Bernardo Arévalo may be a final act of political elites as they try to cling to power.

articles.title: Paraguay, Brazil and Dubai Figure into Massive Transatlantic Cocaine Ring

articles.title: The Perpetual Cocaine War of Colombia’s Putumayo
articles.description: Once a guerrilla stronghold, the southern Colombia coca-growing department of Putumayo is experiencing a new outbreak of violence among warring ex-FARC factions.

articles.title: Crackdown on Guatemala Prosecutors Intensifies Amid Presidential Corruption Claims
articles.description: With cries of corruption mounting against Guatemala’s president, the country’s Attorney General has taken minimal action and instead ramped up her crackdown on prosecutors investigating high-level graft.

articles.title: Despite Peace Agreement, Child Recruitment Plagues Colombia
articles.description: Youth in Colombia continue to be trapped by a range of armed groups, which use everything from bribery to death threats to get them into their ranks, a new report finds.

articles.title: Booming Mexico Resort Town of Puerto Vallarta is Hostage to CJNG
articles.description: Recent US sanctions against an alleged Jalisco Cartel operative have revealed that the powerful Mexican group is using the resort town of Puerto Vallarta to process drug cash through nightclubs, bars and restaurants.

articles.title: Top Ex-Colombian Officers’ Trafficking Case Recalls Past Abuses
articles.description: Three retired Colombian army commanders have been accused of belonging to a criminal network that served the Urabeños drug clan, in a case illustrating how the highest ranks of Colombia’s armed forces continue to be accused of colluding in trafficking activities.

articles.title: Operation Black Tide: The Voyage of a Drug Sub Across the Atlantic
articles.description: When authorities in Spain captured the first known drug submarine to cross the Atlantic, questions soon surfaced about how such a journey could be possible.

articles.title: Cocaine Flows from the Dominican Republic to Puerto Rico
articles.description: Dominican authorities recently intercepted a cocaine-laden speedboat destined for Puerto Rico – the latest in a series of maritime seizures showing a flourishing drug trade between the Dominican Republic and the US territory.

articles.title: Argentina Redraws Marijuana Maps After Massive Seizure
articles.description: A colossal seizure of more than 10,000 cannabis plants in the western provinces of Salta and Jujuy in Argentina provides evidence that marijuana growers are trying to spread the illicit crop to new parts of the country.

articles.title: ‘Invisible’ Drug Trafficker Joins Forces with Former Enemies in Colombia
articles.description: An “invisible” Colombian drug lord whose criminal history dates to the country’s former paramilitary army has been revealed to have set up drug routes with dissident guerrilla fighters, showing how the erstwhile enemies are finding new opportunities to advance mutual trafficking in

articles.title: Former Honduras President Accused of Drug Conspiracy That Moved 500 Tons of Cocaine
articles.description: With the arrest of former Honduras President Juan Orlando Hernández on drug charges, US prosecutors have brought down a powerful figure once considered a US ally, but who later emerged as a lynchpin in what they allege amounted to state-sponsored trafficking.

articles.title: Safehouses, Bodyguards and Fake ID – Panama’s Top Drug Trafficker on the Run
articles.description: The leader of Panama’s largest drug trafficking group has been arrested in Costa Rica and is set to be extradited to the United States, although this is unlikely to heavily disrupt the cocaine pipeline through Central America.

articles.title: What are the Most Corrupt Countries in Latin America?
articles.description: Latin American countries scored poorly on Transparency International’s latest corruption index, with the worst joining the ranks of war-torn nations and dictatorships.

articles.title: Jamaica Hatches New Plan to Seize Illegal Guns as Murders Soar

articles.title: How Brazil’s Gangs Took Their War to Santa Cruz, Bolivia

articles.title: Murders Soar as Venezuela Police Hunt for Wilexis Yet Again
articles.description: A powerful Caracas gang has imposed a curfew after police raids left a dozen people dead – another example of how attempts to root out the gang have led to escalating violence and put residents in danger.

articles.title: Liquid Cocaine: A Rising Trend Among Colombia’s Traffickers
articles.description: As Colombian traffickers and police continually try to outfox each other, liquid cocaine has returned to the fore as an increasingly popular drug smuggling method.

articles.title: US Reveals Former Honduras President’s Place on Central America Corruption List
articles.description: Officials in the United States have revealed that former Honduras President Juan Orlando Hernández is in fact included on a US government list of corrupt actors in Central America, although it remains unclear if this will lead to formal criminal charges.

articles.title: El Koki, Venezuela’s Most Notorious Gang Boss, Shot Dead
articles.description: The story of El Koki, Venezuela’s most notorious gang boss, has ended with his death. After constructing a criminal empire in Caracas which collapsed in 2021, he was shot dead by security forces after months on the run.

articles.title: Bolivia Anti-Drug Chief Target of Both Bolivia and United States
articles.description: The US indictment of Bolivia’s former anti-narcotics chief on drug and weapons charges means he could possibly be extradited to the United States – a move that Bolivia will decide following his arrest there on charges of illicit enrichment.

articles.title: Mexico Migrant Massacre Linked to Sophisticated Guatemala Smuggling Ring

articles.title: Are Arrests of Venezuela Officials Simply For Show?
articles.description: Venezuela’s government has unleashed a new campaign targeting municipal and state officials, police commanders and prosecutors allegedly involved in extortion and drug trafficking, yet its scope raises questions in a country recently listed as the world’s fourth most corrupt.

articles.title: New Minister, But Security Challenges Await Honduras President
articles.description: The return of a former Honduran police chief is the first sign that President Xiomara Castro wants to shake up the country’s security forces – but it will be a difficult task.

articles.title: Lime Crisis in Mexico as Cartels Target Farmers
articles.description: Cartels are known for shakedowns of avocado growers, but lime farmers have been unnoticed victims of similar extortion schemes in Mexico, causing prices to skyrocket.

articles.title: Brazil Tries to Reclaim Rio’s Favelas – Ad Infinitum

articles.description: The death of a top Red Command leader near Rio de Janeiro raises questions about the success of coordinated attempts to weaken one of Brazil’s toughest gangs, both materially and psychologically.

articles.description: Drone attacks in Michoacán, bodies hanging from bridges in Zacatecas, attacks with remotely detonated explosives in Guanajuato, massacres in Jalisco and Tamaulipas, remains scattered across streets in Baja California. These are but some of the scenes seen in the last year and blamed on the CJNG.

articles.title: The Leones’ Revenge – How an Extortion Attempt Went Badly Wrong in Guatemala
articles.description: A settling of scores in Guatemala left at least eight people dead after the son of a long-dead druglord took revenge for the assassination of his wife, again proving how being locked up is no barrier.

articles.title: Cárteles Unidos
articles.description: Cárteles Unidos (CU) es una organización criminal surgida de una alianza entre el Cartel de Tepalcatepec, Los Viagras y otros grupos más para repeler los avances del Cartel Jalisco Nueva Generación (CJNG) en el estado de Michoacán en México. Operan bajo un nombre que antes usaron otros grupos aliado

articles.title: Chepe Diablo, Long Accused Cartel Leader, Let Off the Hook in El Salvador
articles.description: A business magnate who is an alleged leader of El Salvador’s Texis Cartel has escaped justice once again, after a court dropped money laundering charges against him.

articles.title: The Elevated Risks Facing Young Men in Rosario, Argentina
articles.description: Young men make up a quarter of homicide victims in Rosario, according to a new report, driving home the fact that those under 21 are far more likely to join local gangs, work in drug trafficking and be at risk of criminal violence.

articles.title: Convoys of Stolen Wood – Widespread Theft in Chile’s Logging Industry
articles.description: A spate of arrests targeting truck drivers carrying stolen wood have highlighted how the province of Arauco is at the heart of increases in timber trafficking across Chile.

articles.title: Guatemala Drops Charges Against Former President, Arrests Anti-Corruption Prosecutors
articles.description: Guatemala seems to have brushed aside international pressure to crack down on entrenched government corruption, as authorities dropped charges against an embattled former president on the same day they arrested top anti-graft investigators.

articles.title: Brazil’s Environmental Agencies Linked to Trafficking of Amazon Wood
articles.description: The environment minister under investigation. The head of the environmental protection agency suspended. The Brazilian government is facing its strongest accusations to date

articles.title: Impunity Driving Uptick in Violence Against Yanomami in Brazil
articles.description: Sporadic clashes between illegal miners and Indigenous communities are not uncommon in northern Brazil but recent shootouts drive a new point home: the miners are convinced they’ll get away with it.

articles.title: Death of Jesús Santrich Throws Wrench Into Conflict in Venezuela
articles.description: The killing of a notorious Colombian dissident guerrilla leader on Venezuelan soil has the potential to destabilize underworld dynamics in both countries, fueling an already raging conflict along their shared border.

articles.title: Will US-Central America Relations Falter With Latest Corruption List?
articles.description: Though far from revelatory, a new list of officials the United States government suspects of corruption and drug trafficking in El Salvador, Guatemala and Honduras may further roil Washington’s relationships […]

articles.title: Corruption Plagues Argentina’s Justice System
articles.description: A judge in Argentina has been charged with running a corruption network that included lawyers and a slain customs official, in a case that illustrates a pattern of judges accused of orchestrating bribery schemes.

articles.title: Seuxis Pausías Hernández, alias ‘Jesús Santrich’
articles.description: Seuxis Pausías Hernández Solarte, alias “Jesús Santrich,” was a senior former FARC commander who participated in that guerrilla group’s demobilization process before taking up arms again in 2019 to form […]

articles.title: Smugglers Head to the Pacific, Ferrying People From Mexico to the US
articles.description: A boat that capsized off California’s shores carrying some 30 people who paid more than $15,000 each to be shuttled from Mexico to the US offers the latest evidence of how smuggling operations are heading out to sea.

articles.title: The Unlikely Resistance of a Lone Mining Gang in Venezuela
articles.description: The escalating violence between security forces and a gang that formerly enjoyed official protection in Venezuela’s foremost mining region shows how the breakdown of criminal and political alliances can lead to open conflict.

articles.title: Happy Mother’s Day from Your Friendly Neighborhood Cartel Boss
articles.description: In some regions of Mexico, family members who came up empty-handed this Mother’s Day were bailed out by the Jalisco Cartel.

articles.title: Donkey Skin Trafficking on Colombia’s Northern Coast
articles.description: When hundreds of skinned donkeys appeared on Colombia’s northern coast without explanation, locals, and later authorities, started asking questions.

articles.title: Bolivia Family Clans Key Cocaine Suppliers to Brazil, Colombia
articles.description: The extradition of a prominent Bolivian drug trafficker to Brazil has shed rare light on how family clans in the Andean nation serve as important cocaine suppliers to powerful organized crime groups across the region.

articles.title: Dirty Business – The Smuggling Pipeline Carrying Mercury Across the Amazon
articles.description: Smuggling networks are feeding illegal mining operations across the Amazon Basin with mercury, despite global efforts to clamp down on the use of the substance.

articles.title: How Chinese Criminals Secretly Move Millions for Mexico Cartels
articles.description: While the involvement of Chinese money-laundering rings in handling drug proceeds from Mexico is nothing new, a number of recent court cases in the United States have revealed crucial information about how these schemes work.

articles.title: Protests Shatter Already Fragile Image of Colombia’s Security Forces
articles.description: Colombia’s current political crisis has led to severe clashes between police and protesters in many of the country’s largest cities. But the actions carried out by security forces could have a severe impact on their ability to fight criminal groups in the future.

articles.title: Pandemic Spending Immunity Deepens El Salvador Corruption Concerns
articles.description: With a new law granting immunity to El Salvador officials accused of mismanaging coronavirus funds and the resignation of a prosecutor looking into pandemic-related spending, President Nayib Bukele and allies are wasting no time undermining a major corruption investigation aimed at  his administrati

articles.title: Rage, Rinse, Repeat – The Futile Cycle of Anger at Rio’s Police
articles.description: The stranger burst into the house, staggering and leaving a trail of blood behind him. He ran into the back, into the bedroom of the nine-year-old girl. Police were not far behind, screaming to know where he was. They had followed the blood. The children hid behind their mother. The officers went in

articles.title: Weaponized Drones in Mexico: Game-Changer or Gimmick?
articles.description: Every few years, a new spectacular tactic used by criminal groups captures the world’s attention. But like the narco-tank seen in 2011, bomb-strapped drones in Mexico haven’t actually offered much bang for their buck so far.

articles.title: Colombia’s City of Cali Dealing with Fallout of Prosecutor’s Arrest
articles.description: In the past year and a half, two murder cases have shaken the city of Cali: the slaying of its lead organized crime prosecutor and the massacre of five youths in a sugarcane field. Now, the prosecutor leading both has been arrested on charges that he took part in a bribery scheme.

articles.title: Venezuela’s Modeling Agencies Repeatedly Linked to Human Trafficking
articles.description: As sex trafficking has soared in Venezuela, seemingly legitimate modeling agencies are repeatedly being linked to cases of human trafficking, trapping young women and forcing them into sexual servitude.

articles.title: Ousting of El Salvador’s Top Prosecutor Imperils Rule of Law
articles.description: The decision by legislators aligned with El Salvador’s President Nayib Bukele to oust the country’s top prosecutor may spell the end of several crucial organized crime cases.

articles.title: Haiti Massacres Reveal Active Gang Support from Police, Officials
articles.description: A new report by Harvard Law School and a Haitian crime observatory has confirmed government officials and police actively facilitated a series of gang-led attacks in the Caribbean nation, showing how relations between gangs and state actors have evolved.

articles.title: Sinaloa Cartel Princeling Guilty But Group Has Moved On
articles.description: The conviction of Ismael Zambada-Imperial, son of the Sinaloa Cartel’s top leader, in the US may be a headline-grabber but does nothing to blunt the group’s ability to bring cocaine into the country.

articles.title: Poultrygeist – How Mexican Chickens Are Smuggled into Guatemala
articles.description: A series of operations carried out by the Guatemalan Attorney General’s Office against networks dedicated to trafficking Mexican poultry, shed light on how poultry smuggling works in the border area.

articles.title: Brazil’s Cheaper Exotic Fish Still Targeted by Traffickers
articles.description: US and Brazilian authorities have identified an international network dedicated to the smuggling of prized species of killifish, illuminating Brazil’s role in the global trade.

articles.title: Washington Cracks Down on Guatemala Corruption
articles.description: US sanctions on two Guatemalan politicians — one a political operator and the other an influential legislator — are the latest salvo by Washington to show it is serious about taking on corruption in Central America.

articles.title: Brazil’s PCC Scared Rival Kingpin into Surrendering to Police
articles.description: Fahd Jamil Georges, a veteran drug trafficker along the Paraguay-Brazil border, said he surrendered to authorities after being threatened by the PCC — a reminder of how completely the Brazilian gang has come to rule this frontier.

articles.title: End of Lockdown Brings Wave of Cocaine to Puerto Rico
articles.description: Puerto Rico is seeing a surge in cocaine seizures, indicating that drug flows are being reactivated after months of dormancy amid pandemic lockdowns and that the island is on track to tally a record drug haul in 2021.

articles.title: Land Trafficking Fuels Constant Corruption in Bolivia
articles.description: The arrest of an acting government minister in Bolivia has reinforced how land trafficking in the nation is fueled by a strong undercurrent of corruption, time and time again.

articles.title: Libertadores del Vichada
articles.description: The Libertadores del Vichada were a splinter group of the Popular Revolutionary Anti-Terrorist Army of Colombia (ERPAC), based in Colombia’s Eastern Plains. The criminal organization oversaw a substantial drug empire, […]

articles.title: Guatemala’s War Between Morales and CICIG Not Over Yet
articles.description: Former Guatemalan president Jimmy Morales’ dismantling of an anti-corruption commission has come back to haunt him — as prosecutors are asking that he be stripped of immunity to then decide if he committed an illegal act by ousting the commission’s director.

articles.title: Colombia’s Guerrillas Bring Landmines to Venezuela
articles.description: Reports by the Venezuelan government of landmines in Apure state, demonstrate the recent expansion of a warfare tactic used by guerrillas in Colombia into neighboring Venezuela.

articles.title: 4 Security Challenges Awaiting Ecuador’s Next President
articles.description: Ecuador’s next president will face an unprecedented set of security challenges, as prison violence has soared to record levels, the country’s corruption is receiving international attention and the criminal situation along the border with Colombia continues to sour.

articles.title: The Last Ride of an Ally of Pablo Escobar
articles.description: An old associate of Pablo Escobar, who spent 27 years in prison in the United States, is behind bars once again – this time in Peru – which goes to show that older generations of narcos may still be in the mix.

articles.title: Cocaine Spike Puts Spotlight on Honduras Atlantic
articles.description: A string of bumper cocaine seizures on the Honduras Atlantic has provided a stark reminder of the region’s importance in the transnational drug trade.

articles.title: Colombia Teachers – Last Line of Defense Against Forced Recruitment
articles.description: Colombia remains a hotspot for forced recruitment of minors. In this, the last of a three-part investigation by InSight Crime, journalist Mathew Charles looks at how school teachers are trying — unsuccessfully — to thwart this recruitment.

articles.title: What Criminals Plan Via Encrypted Messaging Services
articles.description: The infiltration of an encrypted phone messaging service by Belgian authorities led to a record cocaine haul, showing both how such secretive communications are crucial both to those operating international drug trafficking rings and those seeking to dismantle them.

articles.title: Fentanyl Seizures Explode Across United States
articles.description: Seizures of the deadly synthetic opioid fentanyl by customs agents in the United States have skyrocketed nationwide, underscoring potential shifts in drug markets amid the country’s ongoing opioid crisis.

articles.title: Costa Rica Cocaine Theft Ring Relies on Crooked Agents
articles.description: The dismantling of a Costa Rican drug gang that worked with agents in an elite investigative unit to steal cocaine shipments from rival traffickers is yet another sign of the reach of drug corruption in the Central American country

articles.title: Underwater Drone Would Have Secretly Delivered Cocaine to Europe
articles.description: A successful Detroit telecommunications businessman secretly bankrolled the development and construction of an underwater drone, which could have been used to move tons of cocaine to Europe, according to a recently unsealed US indictment.

articles.title: Ecuador’s Self-Defeating Anti-Trafficking Law
articles.description: While authorities in Ecuador tally record cocaine seizures, a change to the criminal code in the Andean country has possibly made it more difficult to prosecute large-scale traffickers.

articles.title: Are Cartels Connected to Booming Truck Theft in Mexico?
articles.description: Tens of thousands of trucks on Mexico’s highways are being robbed of cargo every year, with criminal gangs becoming more daring, sophisticated and violent when carrying out hijackings.

articles.title: US, Mexico Sanctions Offer Insights Into CJNG Hierarchy
articles.description: Sanctions recently imposed by the United States and Mexico on members of the CJNG that are allegedly linked to two high-profile murders, demonstrate that the group is one of the few remaining hierarchically-structured transnational organizations.

articles.title: US Announcement Against Corrupt Paraguay Official May Signal Shift
articles.description: Recent punitive action taken by the United States against Paraguayan officials has reinforced how far corruption underpins the South American nation’s thriving trade in drugs.

articles.title: In Colombia, Child Soldiers Play Many Roles
articles.description: Children serve in a wide range of roles in criminal organizations: errand boy, landmine builder, frontline combatant and assassin. In this, the second of a three-part investigation by InSight Crime about the recruitment of minors in Colombia by criminal groups and insurgents

articles.title: Bitcoin Cryptocurrency Adds to Venezuela Money Laundering Risk
articles.description: Venezuela is increasingly turning to the cryptocurrency Bitcoin, raising questions about its possible use in laundering money and evading US sanctions, not unlike the way Venezuela already uses the gold trade.

articles.title: Venezuela Kingpin’s Arrest Leaves Power Vacuum on Caribbean Coast
articles.description: Recent drug charges against a little-known but powerful Venezuelan businessman come as a surprise given that he allegedly operated with impunity for decades on the country’s northern coast.

articles.title: Dismantled Network Illustrates Pattern in the Exploitation of Venezuelan Migrants in Colombia
articles.description: A sophisticated human trafficking network dismantled in Colombia, illustrates a pattern in how Venezuelan citizens, particularly women, are recruited.

articles.title: A Large Galápagos Tortoise Seizure Raises Red Flags
articles.description: Authorities in Ecuador have seized close to 200 baby tortoises native to the Galápagos Islands that are coveted in the exotic reptile trade.

articles.title: A Guatemala Arrest, a US Indictment, and a Mexico Attack
articles.description: Following the arrest of a former Mexican mayor in Guatemala, US prosecutors unveiled an indictment against him and criminal rivals allegedly attacked the town he once ruled.

articles.title: In El Salvador, a New Security Minister With a Dubious Past
articles.description: El Salvador President Nayib Bukele has appointed a new security minister whose alleged ties to officials and political operators accused of corruption raise concerns about the government’s commitment to transparent security policy.

articles.title: Bajo Cauca and Vaupés: Colombia’s Child Recruitment Hotspots
articles.description: As in the darkest days of Colombia’s civil conflict, armed groups across the country continue to enlist and exploit teenage boys and girls.  In this, the first of a three-part investigation by InSight Crime about the recruitment of minors in Colombia by criminal groups and insurgents, we explo

articles.title: Jamaica Bets on Security Overhaul to Stem Bloodshed, Improve Anti-Crime Fight
articles.description: After recording the highest murder rate in Latin America and the Caribbean in 2020, Jamaica is already seeing an uptick in violence this year, with an average of more than […]

articles.title: Shining Path Commander’s Death Strikes Blow to Peru Rebel Group
articles.description: Military officials in Peru have announced the death of a top Shining Path commander more than five months after an operation in which they claim he was injured in a […]

articles.title: Uncertainty for Honduras President After Brother’s Life Sentence
articles.description: A judge in the United States sentenced the brother of Honduras President Juan Orlando Hernández to life in prison for drug trafficking, closing one case but increasing tensions between the […]

articles.title: Latin America Elites Seek  Corrupt Access to Vaccines
articles.description: The seizure in Mexico of COVID-19 vaccine smuggled aboard a private plane linked to a Honduran textile magnate marks the first instance of alleged transnational vaccine smuggling in Latin America — in a mysterious case that betrays a pattern of clandestine deals between economic and political

articles.title: Venezuela Sends Mixed Messages to Colombian Insurgents
articles.description: A recent Venezuelan military operation in the state of Apure has once again exposed the complex relationship between the Venezuelan government and Colombia’s insurgent groups camped in the country’s border regions — and more armed clashes should be expected.

articles.title: Police in Brazil Net Mass Seizure of Ornamental Fish
articles.description: Authorities in Brazil have seized thousands of live freshwater fish native to the country’s Amazon region that are commonly seen in the aquarium trade.

articles.title: Child Trafficking Thrives Along Haitian-Dominican Border
articles.description: Recent media reports have revealed that children from Haiti are being trafficked to the Dominican Republic in large numbers, with girls mainly exploited for sex and boys forced to work.

articles.title: Tides Are Turning: First Europe-Built ‘Narco-Sub’ Seized in Spain
articles.description: Authorities in Europe — already overwhelmed by a flood of cocaine arriving in cargo ships — now must face another smuggling method that has bedeviled law enforcement in the Americas: […]

articles.title: Disgruntled Police Sowing Further Unrest in Haiti
articles.description: Officials in Haiti are calling on authorities to break up a group of disaffected, violent police officers that has in recent weeks created chaos in the capital city of Port-au-Prince, […]

articles.title: Sexual Abuse Plagues Women’s Detention Facilities in Venezuela
articles.description: The shooting death of a 19-year-old woman in a Venezuela police holding cell has revealed that women in the jail were being systematically forced to have sex with officers, underscoring the high level of violence faced by women in custody.

articles.title: Deadly Ambush of 13 Police Meets Little Government Response in Mexico
articles.description: A bloody ambush on a police unit in the state of Mexico, in which thirteen officials were shot dead, is exposing the Mexican government’s failure to control escalating violence outside of the capital.

articles.title: Revived Drug Routes, Evolving Street Gangs in El Salvador
articles.description: Though a relatively minor player in the transnational drug trade, El Salvador’s overland smuggling routes have undergone a moderate revival and some factions of the country’s largest street gang — the MS13 — have moved in on territories once controlled by major Salvadoran car

articles.title: Santa Ana, El Salvador
articles.description: Santa Ana is a key transit point for criminal groups smuggling cocaine and marijuana between El Salvador and Guatemala. It also has a strong gang presence.

articles.title: Chalatenango, El Salvador
articles.description: Though not a major hub for transnational organized crime, the department of Chalatenango is a transit point for migrants, arms and drugs.

articles.title: Cabañas, El Salvador
articles.description: Aside from some contraband smuggling between Cabañas and neighboring Honduras, there is no indication that the department is involved in any major transnational crime.

articles.title: San Miguel, El Salvador
articles.description: San Miguel was an important operational center for the once-powerful Perrones drug cartel. It is also a hotbed for gang activity.

articles.title: The Fuel Not the Fuse: Drug Trafficking and Ecuador’s Prison Violence
articles.description: Drug trafficking and transnational organized crime have been blamed for deadly riots among rival gangs in Ecuador’s prisons, but the carnage has more to do with how the country’s gangs intersect with the drug trade.

articles.title: Color-coded Bracelets for Migrants: Separating Poor from Poorer at US-Mexico Border
articles.description: A recent report from the US-Mexico border revealed that human smuggling organizations have begun giving special bracelets to undocumented migrants, a practice that seems to illustrate the different tiers that exist amongst even the most downtrodden and desperate travelers.

articles.title: In Brazil, Court’s Decision Illustrates Corruption Probe Overreach
articles.description: After Brazil’s Supreme Court canceled the conviction of its former president, the country must now contend with the fallout.

articles.title: Los Pachenca Emerge After Return of Paramilitary Boss to Colombia
articles.description: In the wake of former paramilitary leader Hernán Giraldo’s deportation to Colombia, an armed group linked to him has reappeared in a coastal region he once controlled, raising fears of his influence on the criminal landscape.

articles.title: Clans, Corruption and Cross-border Crime in Argentina
articles.description: Argentina’s criminal clans are growing stronger as domestic drug consumption rises, while trafficking networks now thrive in its border regions.

articles.title: Uruguay Remains Hub For Trafficking Women to Europe
articles.description: A collaborative effort by authorities in Uruguay and Spain to dismantle an international sex trafficking network reveals that the South American country remains a source of women trafficked to Europe.

articles.title: Ex-FARC Expand into Northern Colombia
articles.description: A leader of what was once Colombia’s largest guerrilla organization has announced the revival of one of its most formidable blocs in northern Colombia — a region with a competitive criminal landscape.

articles.title: Formosa, Argentina
articles.description: Located on the border with Paraguay’s capital Asunción, the Formosa province is a hub for transnational marijuana trafficking and contraband. Illicit shipments enter the province through the border city of Clorinda, albeit in much smaller quantities than in Argentina’s other northeastern provinces –

articles.title: Misiones, Argentina
articles.description: Misiones is the main entry point for marijuana smuggled from Paraguay into Argentina across the Paraná River and one of the country’s two most important provinces for marijuana trafficking, along with Corrientes.

articles.title: ‘Cartel of the Robe’ Reveals High-Level Corruption in Colombia’s Courts
articles.description: A former chief justice of Colombia’s Supreme Court has been found guilty of corruption in a case that illustrates how the highest levels of judicial power in the country were able to form a criminal union.

articles.title: Will Drug Trial Finally Break US-Honduras Ties?
articles.description: Honduras President Juan Orlando Hernández once again stole the limelight in a major drug trafficking trial in the United States, raising serious doubts about future cooperation between the countries.

articles.title: Familial Fiefdom: Ocós, Guatemala and the Preciado Navarijo Legacy
articles.description: The extradition of a mayor threatens to topple his family’s decades-long political control of a coastal Pacific town — a region where local officials have a history of drug trafficking.

articles.title: Cartel Violence in Central Mexico Taking Hold in Jerez
articles.description: Violence in the town of Jerez, in Mexico’s central state of Zacatecas, has surged in 2021, as criminal groups fight for control of key drug transit and production zones.

articles.title: Massacres Spike in Antioquia as Colombia’s Gaitanistas Expand
articles.description: The central Colombian department of Antioquia has been the scene of four massacres over the first two months of 2021, as the Urabeños trafficking group aims to expand both its routes to the Pacific coast and local drug markets.

articles.title: Battle for Guatemala’s Top Courts Intensifies
articles.description: The naming of judges to Guatemala’s high courts has turned into an escalating fight between political elites seeking to stack the courts with allies and anti-impunity prosecutors accusing the elites of influence peddling.

articles.title: How Brazil’s Borders Became More Diverse, Dangerous
articles.description: Criminal dynamics in Brazil, particularly its drug trafficking routes, have been shaken up in recent years by the rapid expansion of the PCC.

articles.title: Stay Frosty: Further Innovations in Drug Trafficking
articles.description: Corn flakes “frosted” in cocaine and fentanyl pills hidden inside toys might make for amusing headlines, but these recent discoveries demonstrate how commonplace the smuggling of drugs inside consumer goods has become.

articles.title: Paraná, Brazil
articles.description: Paraná is a hub for drug trafficking. Vast quantities of cocaine and marijuana enter the state from Paraguay, via the Paraná River, and from Foz do Iguaçu.

articles.title: Rio Grande do Sul, Brazil
articles.description: Strategically located between Argentina and Uruguay, Rio Grande do Sul is a hub for contraband and drug smuggling.

articles.title: Colectivos Ramp Up Property Seizures in Venezuela
articles.description: An increase in the number of reports of illegal invasions of homes and commercial establishments in Venezuela during the pandemic has revealed the ever-widening criminal portfolio managed by militant civilian groups known as “colectivos.”

articles.title: Belize Officials, Police Help Hide Drug Planes
articles.description: A recent drug plane interception in Belize has unveiled the role corrupt officials have been playing in facilitating the nation’s use as a transit point for cocaine.

articles.title: Latin America’s Bustling Trade in Seahorses to China
articles.description: A new investigation has identified Mexico as a major contributor to a global seahorse trafficking network centered around China but it seems likely that this trade is also affecting other Latin American nations.

articles.title: Europe Cocaine Hauls Reach New Record Heights
articles.description: While the seizure of 23 tons of cocaine by German, Belgian and Dutch police, the largest-ever haul of the drug in the EU, was noteworthy, it serves as merely the latest proof of how Europe has become the epicenter of the global cocaine trade.

articles.title: Paraguay Grapples with Evolving Crime, Persistent Corruption
articles.description: Paraguay has long been a key link in South America’s drug trafficking chain, but has grown in importance with the emergence of new criminal players and economies.

articles.title: Drug Trafficking and Political Protection in Paraguay: The Case of ‘Cucho’ Cabaña
articles.description: In a Paraguay border state, drug traffickers and dirty politicians find common ground, and political fights spill into the underworld.

articles.title: Venezuela Oil Crisis May Set Limits on PDVSA Impunity
articles.description: Venezuelan officials may have finally met a PDVSA corruption scandal they cannot allow to go unpunished, hinting at just how dire the country’s oil and gas shortage has become.

articles.title: Concepción, Paraguay
articles.description: In Concepción, powerful drug traffickers have penetrated local politics. With corruption rife, some municipalities have started to resemble criminal enclaves.

articles.title: Itapúa, Paraguay
articles.description: Itapúa is a minor marijuana producer but an important transit corridor for cannabis smuggled into Argentina. Some areas of the river border it shares with Argentina are considered hotspots for marijuana trafficking by authorities in both countries.

articles.title: Boquerón, Paraguay
articles.description: Boquerón is an important part of regional cocaine trafficking routes due to its strategic location on Paraguay’s border with Bolivia and its vast stretches of unpopulated land.

articles.title: Presidente Hayes, Paraguay
articles.description: Presidente Hayes is one of three departments in Paraguay’s Chaco region, a vast and inhospitable forest area, where criminal organizations operate with relative impunity.

articles.title: Amambay, Paraguay
articles.description: Amambay is at the heart of Paraguay’s most worrying organized crime trends: cocaine trafficking, marijuana production, and the rise of violent criminal groups.

articles.title: Misiones, Paraguay
articles.description: Misiones does not play an important role in regional criminal dynamics, despite some marijuana trafficking in the department.

articles.title: Asunción, Paraguay
articles.description: Paraguay’s capital is a transit point for Bolivian cocaine shipped through the Tri-Border area, bound for Brazil. The Paraguay River, which passes through Asunción, also serves as a smuggling route for cocaine and marijuana shipments heading south into Argentina.

articles.title: Venezuela’s Saab Story Continues With Benefit Concert
articles.description: As alleged Maduro financier Alex Saab awaits potential extradition to the United States, the Venezuelan government decided that a concert catered by a company linked to Saab’s illicit activities would be an effective way to try and secure his release.

articles.title: US Senators Seek Sanctions Against Honduras President
articles.description: Eight Democratic senators have sent the first signals that the United States will divorce itself from Honduras President Juan Orlando Hernández amid mounting allegations of drug trafficking, saying in a proposed sanctions bill that there is substantial evidence he has engaged in a pattern of crimina

articles.title: Emma Coronel Aided ‘El Chapo’ Escape, Sinaloa Cartel Drug Trafficking: US
articles.description: The indictment of the wife of convicted Sinaloa Cartel kingpin Joaquín “El Chapo” Guzmán Loera has revealed her alleged role in the organization, ranging from her husband’s messenger to coordinating his spectacular jailbreaks, yet seemingly always one step removed from the drug trafficking its

articles.title: El Koki’s Ambition Fuels Urban Warfare in Caracas
articles.description: Recent shootouts between security forces and Venezuela’s El Coqui gang included a clash in which at least two dozen people were gunned down, and experts warn of continued violence as the gang tries to advance on new territory.

articles.title: Minor Players Take Center Stage in Guatemala Drug Trade
articles.description: An anti-narcotics operation targeting an alleged trafficking ring in Guatemala has shed light on the increasing importance of small, often discrete transport networks in the country’s cocaine trade.

articles.title: Hide and Seek: How Drug Traffickers Get Creative at Sea
articles.description: Drug traffickers engage in a creative game of hide and seek with coast guards and other security forces that board their ships at sea.

articles.title: FARC Victims Claim Compensation from Venezuelan State Oil Company
articles.description: A US court case seeks to compensate victims of the FARC with assets seized from Venezuelan state oil company PDVSA, in the latest push by US prosecutors to establish legal responsibility of entities linked to the Venezuelan state for the actions of the Colombian guerrilla group.

articles.title: Guatemala’s Los Huistas Continue to Build Influence Network
articles.description: Los Huistas, a Guatemalan drug trafficking group connected to a number of government officials, has continued to build its network of political influence, as recent events have shown.

articles.title: One Party, Many Crimes: The Case of Honduras’ National Party
articles.description: The party that has governed Honduras since 2010 has become a federation that welcomes politicians and officials involved in criminal businesses ranging from timber to drug trafficking to the misappropriation of public funds.

articles.title: Cortés, Honduras
articles.description: Cortés is a major organized crime hub. Vast quantities of drugs, arms, and contraband pass through the department’s busy Atlantic port – Puerto Cortés – on a daily basis.

articles.title: Copán, Honduras
articles.description: In Copán – a major transit point for cocaine – drug trafficking groups collaborate with local authorities to smuggle narcotics over the department’s porous western border with Guatemala.

articles.title: Intibucá, Honduras
articles.description: Intibucá is not a major organized crime hub, though there is a notable gang presence along the department’s porous southern border with El Salvador and some signs of drug trafficking.

articles.title: Ocotepeque, Honduras
articles.description: Corruptible customs officials and porous borders facilitate the smuggling of migrants and drugs from Ocotepeque into Guatemala, along with flows of arms and contraband to and from adjacent departments in both Guatemala and El Salvador.

articles.title: Santa Bárbara, Honduras
articles.description: Santa Bárbara sees its fair share of narcotics seizures and has played host to major drug trafficking groups such as the Valle Clan.

articles.title: Lempira, Honduras
articles.description: Lempira is not a major hub for organized criminal activity but is nonetheless an important transit point for illicit narcotics and a political power center for National Party politicians involved in cocaine trafficking.

articles.title: Biden and Central America’s Anti-Corruption Crusade
articles.description: Hopes are high in the Northern Triangle, that the arrival of Joseph Biden and his administration to the White House will lead to the creation of a regional commission that helps local prosecutors investigate major corruption cases. However, launching such a commission is not so simple.

articles.title: Keyla Martínez and Extrajudicial Killings in Honduras
articles.description: The violent death of nursing student Keyla Patricia Martínez Rodríguez while in police custody in Honduras recalls the worst accusations of brutality and impunity that have beset the country’s National Police for decades.

articles.title: The United States, a Special Operations Unit and a Massacre in Mexico
articles.description: US authorities at both the federal and state level provided training to members of a Mexican special operations unit with a history of alleged human rights abuses that was recently implicated in the gruesome massacre of 19 mostly Guatemalan migrants.

articles.title: Venezuela’s Other Plight: Sex Trafficking in Trinidad and Tobago
articles.description: Ongoing interceptions targeting sex trafficking rings operating between Venezuela and Trinidad and Tobago have revealed the extent to which Venezuelan women and minors are being continually smuggled to the island nation from critical hubs

articles.title: Top Mexico Tax Official Fired for Permitting Money Laundering
articles.description: Mexico’s tax authority has dismissed Ramón García Gibson, one of its highest-ranking officials, for “evident conflicts of interest” and his participation in a scheme that enabled the Sinaloa and Norte de Valle cartels to use HSBC Mexico’s infrastructure to launder hundreds of millions of dolla

articles.title: Mexico’s Tourist Corridor: Dream Destination for Drug Traffickers
articles.description: A recent string of high-profile drug plane interceptions suggests the once tranquil Mexican state of Quintana Roo is being increasingly relied upon as a drug trafficking hub.

articles.title: Honduras President Targeted Again in US Criminal Investigation
articles.description: Federal prosecutors have specified in a recently filed court document that Honduran President Juan Orlando Hernández has been under investigation for connections to drug trafficking, though US authorities have targeted him in the past.

articles.title: Human Trafficking Accompanies Illegal Mining in Venezuela’s Orinoco
articles.description: A new report on human trafficking in Venezuela’s Bolívar state shows that forced labor camps and sex trafficking rings are rampant in the region’s illegal mines.

articles.title: The Rise of “Plata” over “Plomo:” The Cancer of Corruption
articles.description: In July 2020, British drug trafficker Robert Dawes was sentenced to 22 years in prison for his role in one of the most emblematic European drug trafficking cases of the last decade: the attempt to smuggle almost 1.4 tons of cocaine on an Air France flight from Caracas to Paris.

articles.title: Cocaine: The Criminal Steroid
articles.description: Cocaine is a criminal steroid. Those that gain access to its riches enjoy accelerated growth and power – quickly – usually leaving a trail of violence and corruption in their wake.

articles.title: The Italian Mafia and the Move Upstream
articles.description: Operation ‘Tiburón Galloway’ began as a local investigation by prosecutors in the Italian region of Calabria in 2001, but quickly snowballed into a multinational, multiagency investigation.

articles.title: Spain: The European Base for Latin American Organized Crime
articles.description: On April 30, 1984, the Mercedes taking Colombian Justice Minister Rodrigo Lara Bonilla home after work was strafed with machine gun fire.

articles.title: The Colombian Cocaine Shift to Europe: the Business No-Brainer
articles.description: In 1989, Los Angeles police transformed Europe’s cocaine trade when they broke open a padlock guarding a Californian warehouse.

articles.title: The Little-Known Bolivia-Asia Drug Route
articles.description: The seizure of a cocaine-laced package, bound for Thailand, at an airport in Bolivia, shines light on the little-known drug trafficking route to south and southeastern Asia that relies on commercial flights out of Bolivia.

articles.title: InSight Crime Presents Guatemala Cross-Border Crime and Corruption Investigation
articles.description: Drug trafficking has been reconfigured in Guatemala. The large clans that traditionally dominated the business have broken up.

articles.title: US-Trained Police Implicated in Mexico Migrant Massacre
articles.description: A dozen police officers have been implicated in the massacre of 19 people along the US-Mexico border at the end of January, underscoring the systematic violence directed at those most vulnerable in areas where organized crime groups operate.

articles.title: InSight Crime’s 2020 Homicide Round-Up
articles.description: While unrest gripped much of Latin America in 2019, it was the coronavirus that took center stage and ripped through the region in 2020, upending everything from commercial trade to the operations of local gangs and transnational criminal organizations.

articles.title: A Mayor and a Wave of Narco Violence on Guatemala’s Pacific
articles.description: The mayor of Moyuta, Guatemala, knows how to do politics and business along the rough and tumble Guatemalan-Salvadoran border.

articles.title: Migrants Killed in Mexico Took Treacherous Path
articles.description: Nineteen people shot and burned in a Mexico border state near Texas is a macabre reminder that migrants being smuggled to the United States are targets of cartel violence.

articles.title: Haiti President Calls for Aid as Gang Kidnappings Surge
articles.description: The president of Haiti is urging citizens to work together with the Caribbean nation’s police force to help quell an alarming uptick in gang kidnappings for ransom, highlighting the state’s inability to weather the fallout from backing increasingly powerful criminal actors.

articles.title: Decades of Deforestation, Illegal Mining and Impunity in Nicaragua
articles.description: Nicaragua’s Indio Maíz Biological Reserve, on the southern border with Costa Rica, is under assault.

articles.title: Cachiros Money Laundering Cells Still Operate in Honduras
articles.description: Authorities in Honduras have revealed in recent months million-dollar money laundering operations connected to the Cachiros, indicating that vestiges of the drug clan continue to operate in the country despite the group’s demise.

articles.title: New Houses, Same Violence: Argentina’s Public Housing Challenge
articles.description: In Buenos Aires, a shootout between gangs in a public housing development has highlighted a deficiency in Argentina’s public housing initiative: without complimentary security policies, public housing projects offer gangs fresh territory to expand illicit operations, perpetuating social insecurity.

articles.title: Chiquimula, Guatemala
articles.description: Chiquimula is a transit point and hub for drug trafficking. It also has marijuana production and a vibrant contraband and illegal mining market.

articles.title: Jutiapa, Guatemala
articles.description: Jutiapa is a transit point for narcotics shipments and a hotspot for illegal firearm possession. Criminal groups smuggle illicit weapons into the department via its porous eastern border with El Salvador. Jutiapa consistently ranks among the Guatemalan departments with the highest rates of illicit w

articles.title: Bolivia Rivers Become Increasingly Popular Drug Trafficking Routes
articles.description: Drug trafficking routes are starting to appear along rivers and canals in Bolivia, threatening to complicate efforts to stop the flow of cocaine to international markets.

articles.title: 3 Unexplored Criminal Challenges Awaiting President Joe Biden
articles.description: President Joe Biden’s policy documents on Latin America tread familiar ground. But the region contains a range of other security and criminal threats that are of direct concern to the United States.

articles.title: Trump Leaves Biden Corruption Problem at Mexico Border Wall
articles.description: Former President Donald Trump’s border wall project has developed a spotty record of attracting organized crime to its many construction sites, leaving the incoming Biden administration with a financial, legal […]

articles.title: No Time to Wait: Biden’s Security Challenges in Central America
articles.description: Given the challenges of a deadly pandemic, President Joe Biden could be forgiven for not having Central America’s security at the top of his agenda on day one, or 100 for that matter.

articles.title: Mexico Clears Ex-Defense Minister, Accuses US of ‘Fabricating’ Drug Charges
articles.description: Authorities in Mexico said they will not pursue criminal charges against a former defense minister who US prosecutors accused of colluding with organized crime groups to traffic drugs, only for them to drop those charges when security ties between the two countries showed signs of fraying.

articles.title: Human Smuggling Thrives Between Costa Rica and Nicaragua
articles.description: Recent interceptions linked to the illicit smuggling of migrants at Nicaragua’s border with Costa Rica have illustrated the extent to which clandestine groups and individuals dedicated to the trade continue to operate between both nations, despite mobility restrictions associated with the coronaviru

articles.title: Mexico’s Fentanyl Crisis Reached New Heights in 2020
articles.description: Trafficking of the deadly opioid fentanyl skyrocketed in 2020 throughout Mexico, solidifying the synthetic drug’s status as a top criminal economy and the country’s role as an international trafficking transit point.

articles.title: Fredy Nájera, Fixer of Narco-Politics in Honduras
articles.description: It was in 2012 that former Honduran congressman Fredy Renán Nájera Montoya agreed to provide Mexico’s powerful Sinaloa Cartel with the connections to traffic cocaine through Puerto Cortés, a large port on the northern Caribbean coast. Six years later, he was still a state representative in good stan

articles.title: Lorenzanas
articles.description: The Lorenzanas are a traditional contraband family, who, over time, became enmeshed in the lucrative drug trade. The group’s criminal career ended in the early 2010s with the arrest and extradition of its top leaders.

articles.title: Allegations Against Honduras President Add to Narco-State Case
articles.description: With new accusations that Honduras President Juan Orlando Hernández directly brokered deals to protect traffickers in exchange for drug money, the president himself has emerged as a lynchpin in Honduras’ descent into a narco-state.

articles.title: Rio Militias and Their Complex Empire of Legal Businesses
articles.description: As part of a mounting campaign going after criminal finances, police in Rio de Janeiro have asked courts to seize control of legal businesses used by militia groups to launder money.

articles.title: Middleman Linked Colombian Gangs with Israeli, Japanese Mafia
articles.description: An Israeli national suspected of ties to Japanese and Israeli mafia groups has been accused of running drug trafficking, money laundering, and sex trafficking operations in Colombia, revealing the logistical role of foreign brokers in organized crime in the Andean nation.

articles.title: Why Courtroom Corruption Has Costa Rica Concerned
articles.description: A string of recent arrests connecting judicial officials to drug trafficking organizations in Costa Rica have raised concerns that institutional corruption facilitating the trade is becoming more commonplace, as a record amount of cocaine flows through the country.

articles.title: Albanian Mafia Leaves Trail of Blood in Ecuador
articles.description: The assassination of Albanian national Adriatik Tresa inside his luxury property in Ecuador has revealed details of his alleged criminal activities, painting a picture of violent Albanian drug emissaries operating in the Andean country.

articles.title: Mexico City’s Roaring Trade in Wildlife Trafficking
articles.description: A series of unusual and contrasting seizures of rare fauna in the Mexico City area have raised important questions about the modus operandi of wildlife trafficking in and through Mexico’s capital, long regarded as a key regional hub of the illegal wildlife trade.

articles.title: From Hope to Uncertainty: Venezuelans in Far-Right Chile
articles.description: In January, Venezuelans in Chile briefly celebrated the fall of Nicolás Maduro, but many now fear a harsher climate for migrants as the country shifts to the right.

articles.title: The FBI’s Return to Ecuador Is Nothing New
articles.description: The announcement of a permanent FBI office in Quito is the latest iteration of the U.S. using security cooperation to intervene in the country’s domestic politics.

articles.title: Milei’s War against the Working Class
articles.description: Argentina’s sweeping labor reform, heavily resisted in the streets, is set to roll back nearly a century of hard-fought rights.

articles.title: Gang Violence and the Defense of Impunity in Guatemala
articles.description: The Barrio 18’s January attacks sought to destabilize the country to benefit well-connected political elites who have challenged the government of President Bernardo Arévalo.

articles.title: The Donroe Coalition | Under the Shadow Season 2, Episode 8
articles.description: Last weekend, Donald Trump met with right-wing allies from Latin America to kick off a new military coalition to attack so-called drug cartels and push his agenda up and down the hemisphere.

articles.title: Faith and Body: New Battle Over Abortion in Argentina
articles.description: Reproductive rights in Argentina face increased obstacles as the administration of Javier Milei emboldens anti-rights groups and politicians.

articles.title: 38 Londres Street: On Impunity, Pinochet in England, and a Nazi in Patagonia (Review)
articles.description: Philipe Sands’ book on the attempts to hold a Nazi leader and Chilean dictator accountable provide lessons for today’s era in which the flouting of international law has become routine.

articles.title: The Rise of Rodriguismo in Costa Rica
articles.description: Amidst rising fears of insecurity, Costa Ricans elected Laura Fernández, a continuity candidate whose predecessor presided over a political project that breaks with the country’s democratic traditions.

articles.title: An Unbowed Petro Successfully Plays the Diplomat
articles.description: Colombian President Gustavo Petro’s positive meeting with Trump stands to benefit not only his coalition, but also the region writ large.

articles.title: “Luciérnagas en El Mozote” And The Battle Over Historical Memory in El Salvador
articles.description: A state-backed film detailing the 1981 massacre at El Mozote raises thorny questions about the country’s civil war as the victims continue fighting for justice.

articles.title: When the Bombs Fell on Caracas | Under the Shadow Season 2, Episode 5
articles.description: The United States has painted its January 3 invasion of Venezuela as a surgical operation. But for those on the ground it was a disaster. The US killed more than 100 people. It hit civilian targets and left terror and destruction in its wake.

articles.title: Bolivia frente a un nuevo tiempo de luchas
articles.description: Tras una serie de protestas por recortes en los subsidios al combustible que paralizaron el país, los poderosos movimientos sociales de Bolivia abren un nuevo capítulo y se enfrentan a nuevas formas de represión.

articles.title: Maduro Was “Captured,” Our Campus Doesn’t Have to Be
articles.description: As a right-wing center harnesses public funds to bolster U.S. imperial power at Florida International University (FIU), a faculty member issues an urgent call to resist.

articles.title: Bolivians Enter a New Era of Popular Struggles
articles.description: After a series of protests over cuts to fuel subsidies brought the country to a halt, Bolivia’s powerful social movements embark on a new chapter and face new forms of repression.

articles.title: El significado global del ataque de Estados Unidos a Venezuela
articles.description: Más allá de la apropiación de los recursos petroleros y minerales de Venezuela, la invasión del 3 de enero es parte de la crisis de época del capitalismo global.

articles.title: Las amenazas contra un triunfo LGBTQ+ en República Dominicana
articles.description: A pesar de reciente despenalización de las relaciones sexuales entre personas del mismo sexo en las fuerzas armadas y la policía, la decisión se enfrenta a ataques por parte del gobierno de derecha y sus aliados.

articles.title: An LGBTQ+ Win in the Dominican Republic Comes Under Fire
articles.description: Despite the recent decriminalization of same-sex relationships in the armed forces and police, the ruling is being attacked by the right-wing government and its allies.

articles.title: The Global Meaning of the U.S. Attack on Venezuela
articles.description: Beyond the seizure of Venezuela’s oil and mineral resources, the January 3 invasion is inscribed within the epochal crisis of global capitalism.

articles.title: In Paraguay, NGOs are Under Attack
articles.description: Echoing a global trend, the country’s ruling Colorado Party has relied on vague accusations of corruption to attack critical NGOs and further their far-right agenda.

articles.title: The Americas Will Never Be the Same | Season 2, Ep. 2
articles.description: Early on January 3, the U.S. government invaded Venezuela and took President Maduro into custody. It marked the first time the United States used its own military to invade a South American country.

articles.title: Chile at a Crossroads: Human Rights and the Environment Under Kast
articles.description: Chile’s new far-right president seeks to recycle the military regime’s political project, risking hard earned advances in human, Indigenous, and environmental rights.

articles.title: Un nuevo capítulo absurdo en la “guerra contra las drogas”
articles.description: Las ejecuciones extrajudiciales de pescadores latinoamericanos y el indulto a un expresidente y narcotraficante hondureño revelan las letales contradicciones de la política estadounidense de la “guerra contra las drogas”.

articles.title: Barbecue, Empire, and Haiti’s Child Soldiers
articles.description: Haiti’s gang coalition Viv Ansanm is powered by thousands of child combatants drawn by hunger, displacement, and a century of foreign intervention.

articles.title: The Secret Agent (Review)
articles.description: Kleber Mendonça Filho’s latest political thriller, O agente secreto, comes at the end of an exceptional year for Brazilian films set during the military dictatorship.

articles.title: Impunity and Infighting Reign in Bolivia
articles.description: As the country shifts to the right and its new leaders fight amongst themselves, justice and reparations for the 2019 massacres come to a halt.

articles.title: Trump’s War on Venezuelans: From Operation Aurora to Operation Southern Spear
articles.description: How a Colorado slumlord’s lie about Venezuelan gangs was leveraged by the Trump administration to detain thousands, deploy warships, and rewrite the rules of immigration enforcement.

articles.title: Ecuador rechaza el giro derechista de Noboa
articles.description: Los ecuatorianos votaron en contra de una  agenda de gobierno alineada con Estados Unidos, defendieron las protecciones constitucionales progresistas y rechazaron el retorno de bases militares extranjeras.

articles.title: Ecuador Rejects Noboa’s Rightward Turn
articles.description: Ecuadorians vote against the government’s U.S.-aligned agenda, defending progressive constitutional protections and opposing foreign military bases.

articles.title: As Detention Surges Under Trump, Organizers Build a Lasting Movement
articles.description: As the president pumps $45 billion into ICE over the next three years, activists and communities must balance rapid response with longer-term resistance.

articles.title: Narcofemicide in Argentina: When State Absence Meets Patriarchal Violence
articles.description: A brutal triple femicide exposes how women’s bodies are the battlefield of a war the authorities refuse to name.

articles.title: On Peru’s Frontline, Ordinary Citizens Face Extraordinary Violence
articles.description: The dedicated protesters who make up the “primera línea” are fighting for a new Peru—and confronting lethal state repression.

articles.title: Antes de las elecciones, corrupción y una crisis económica plagan la administración de Milei
articles.description: Una red de sobornos en la que está involucrada la hermana del presidente argentino, Karina Milei, es solo otro de la serie de escándalos que rodean al gobierno de Milei en vísperas de las elecciones legislativas.

articles.title: Before Elections, Corruption and Economic Crisis Plague Milei
articles.description: A kickback scheme involving the Argentine President’s sister, Karina Milei, is just another in a string of scandals surrounding Milei’s administration in the lead-up to legislative elections.

articles.title: La Generación Z del Perú ayuda a derrocar a la presidenta
articles.description: Inspirados por una ola global de protestas, los jóvenes manifestantes de Perú han convertido la indignación en redes sociales en una movilización masiva contra la corrupción y la represión.

articles.title: Peru’s Gen Z Helps Bring Down the President
articles.description: Inspired by a global wave of protest, Peru’s youth protesters have turned social media outrage into mass mobilization against corruption and repression.

articles.title: Ecuador’s Paro Protests Autocracy, Not Diesel
articles.description: President Noboa escalates repression and lawfare against protesters, targeting Indigenous people and water and land defenders.

articles.title: Impunity and a Splintered MAS pave the way for the Right’s Return in Bolivia
articles.description: As the country heads to an October 19 runoff between two conservative candidates, any hope of justice for the victims of right-wing violence fades.

articles.title: El Congreso revoca los vetos de Milei: victoria para la salud y la educación
articles.description: Los vetos de Milei a la financiación de las universidades públicas y la salud han sido revocados por ambas cámaras del Congreso, una victoria histórica para la democracia en Argentina.

articles.title: Congress Overturns Milei’s Vetoes: Victory for Healthcare and Education
articles.description: Milei’s vetoes of funding for public universities and healthcare overturned by both houses of Congress, marking a historic win for democracy in Argentina.

articles.title: Colombia’s Peace Court Delivers Historic Sentence Against Military Officers
articles.description: The ruling by the transitional justice tribunal, though criticized by some victims, brings hope that the country may soon turn the page.

articles.title: Bolsonaro’s Conviction and the Fight Against Amnesty in Brazil
articles.description: A collective of descendants of former political prisoners weighs in on the historic ruling and the ongoing struggle for truth, justice, and accountability.

articles.title: Re-militarization of Puerto Rico Exacerbates Environmental Injustice
articles.description: In the height of hurricane season, U.S. military operations in Puerto Rico worsen environmental racism. Meanwhile, communities fight for rooftop solar access for all.

articles.title: Por qué fracasó el primer laboratorio legal de coca en Colombia
articles.description: Una comunidad indígena Nasa quiso darle a la hoja de coca un uso distinto al narcotráfico. Pero el proyecto se desplomó bajo el peso de la presión estadounidense y la prohibición internacional.

articles.title: Panama’s President Cracks Down on Unions
articles.description: Amidst protests, President José Raúl Mulino holds firm to social security reform while suspending habeas corpus and attacking unions and striking workers.

articles.title: Jubilados y jubiladas en Argentina lideran la lucha contra el programa de austeridad de Milei
articles.description: La violencia estatal sin precedentes contra los jubilados y sus aliados no ha logrado sofocar el descontento popular por la creciente precariedad de la vida en Argentina.

articles.title: Struggling Pensioners Lead the Charge Against Milei’s Austerity Agenda
articles.description: Unprecedented state violence against retirees and their allies has failed to quash popular discontent at the increasing precarity of life in Argentina.

articles.title: Dissident Peace: Autonomous Struggles and the State in Colombia (Review)
articles.description: Anthony Dest’s Dissident Peace is a study of political possibilities in Colombia in a period that begins with the signing of the 2016 Peace Accords between the Revolutionary Armed Forces […]

articles.title: Echoes of the Water War: Legacies of Cochabamba, Bolivia (Review)
articles.description: A newly expanded edition of Oscar Olivera’s book revisits the grassroots uprising that defeated water privatization 25 years later, offering lessons on sovereignty, solidarity, and defense of the commons.

articles.title: Uribe on Trial: Colombia’s Justice System Faces Its Defining Test
articles.description: In a nation long plagued by impunity, the historic conviction of Colombia’s powerful former president is testing the country’s judicial independence and raising the stakes for truth and accountability.

articles.title: The Militarization of Commercial Activity in Mexico
articles.description: After building the Tren Maya, Mexico’s military has expanded its commercial reach in the Yucatán, with no regard for transparency or accountability.

articles.title: Colombia desafía la impunidad israelí desde arriba y desde abajo
articles.description: Mientras el presidente Petro intensifica sus esfuerzos para detener las exportaciones de carbón a Israel, los activistas presionan para que se tomen medidas decisivas.

articles.title: Dictatorship Across Borders: Brazil, Chile and the South American Cold War (Review)
articles.description: Burns’s book brings South American history to the fore, explaining the regional dynamics and economic turmoil that led to Salvador Allende’s downfall and the beginnings of Operation Condor.

articles.title: Colombia Challenges Israeli Impunity from Above and Below
articles.description: As President Petro ramps up efforts to halt coal exports to Israel, activists push for decisive action and chart a new path for Global South solidarity.

articles.title: Suriname Chooses New President, Considers New Paths
articles.description: The election of the country’s first woman president comes after years of corruption and economic insecurity.

articles.title: Yon Sosa. Historia del MR13 en Guatemala y México: Seguida de las Memorias Militares del Comandante Guerrillero (REVIEW)
articles.description: Arturo Taracena’s deep dive into the Guatemalan guerrilla group and its leader, informed by personal experience and decades of research, is social history at its finest.

articles.title: Panama Fights for Freedom
articles.description: Anger at neoliberal austerity, Canadian mining corruption, and U.S. imperialism have converged into a powerful protest movement.
